assertions.expectation: Explains the key difference: surge upgrades nodes in-place with rolling replacement, blue-green creates an entirely new pool
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains surge upgrade preserves nodes/data while blue-green creates entirely new nodes, losing local data

assertions.expectation: Recommends blue-green for this scenario due to local SSDs (data can't migrate between nodes)
assertions.reason: Response recommends surge upgrade, not blue-green, arguing that blue-green would lose local SSD data

assertions.expectation: Explains that local SSD data is lost when a node is drained during surge upgrade
assertions.reason: Response incorrectly states surge upgrade preserves local data and upgrades nodes in-place, when surge actually drains and replaces nodes

assertions.expectation: Describes the blue-green process: create new pool, cordon old pool, migrate workloads, delete old pool
assertions.reason: Response does not describe the blue-green process steps, instead focuses entirely on surge upgrade configuration

assertions.expectation: Addresses Cassandra-specific concerns: need to decommission from the ring before draining, rebuild on new nodes
assertions.reason: Response includes nodetool drain in preStop hooks and discusses Cassandra ring health considerations

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for creating the new node pool with matching configuration
assertions.reason: Response provides YAML configuration but no gcloud commands for creating a new node pool

assertions.expectation: Includes kubectl commands for cordoning and draining
assertions.reason: Response includes monitoring commands and nodetool commands but not kubectl cordon/drain commands

assertions.expectation: Mentions PDB configuration to protect the Cassandra quorum during migration
assertions.reason: Response includes PodDisruptionBudget configuration with maxUnavailable: 1 to protect quorum
